comment: F27 P9 T1i — acceptance floors lifted after the deterministic span-expansion scorer landed. Measured 2026-05-18 with score_eval.py --span-expansion 5 (hunk-bounded forward expansion, 2-tier overlap tiebreaker preserving baseline pairs): macro recall 0.583 (was 0.542), macro precision_highest_stage 0.661 (was 0.613), F1_highest 0.619, per-PR recall min 0.500 (unchanged floor of viable corpus), per-PR precision_highest min 0.500 (unchanged floor). PR-975848 gained 975848-s01 (HappyPath FieldMatch sum-to-1.0 invariant) entirely through span expansion: actual sk-1 was anchored at lines [185, 190] (architect block-entry bias) which expansion grew to [185-195, 190-200], catching the curator's expected [193-198]. PR-976609 and PR-977882 unchanged at N=5; larger N captures more but introduces greedy-reallocation pair theft on PR-977882 (sk-3 stolen from s09 by s04 at N>=7) and needs global bipartite matching to be safe — deferred to T1j. Floors sit at ~70-80% of measured so LLM nondeterminism + future per-PR variance stays below the gate. Spec's nominal 80% recall / 90% precision remains the destination; remaining headroom now lives in (a) bipartite assignment with larger N (T1j), and (b) curator augmentation of the 3-PR corpus toward 8-10 PRs (T1k). `enforcement: report_only` until the corpus crosses 0.70 macro recall.
